subject: DevOps
title: Dependency Pinning and Lock Files
description: A fast breakdown of Dependency Pinning and Lock Files for builders.

hook.text: A package updated overnight with malware. Every morning build pulled the infected code silently.

buildup.text: Unpinned deps use version ranges. A compromised release gets pulled in without anyone noticing.

discovery.text: Lock files pin exact versions and hashes. Your build uses the exact same code you tested last time.

twist.text: Even with lock files, audit updates. Dependabot PRs should be reviewed, not auto-merged blindly.

climax.text: npm audit, pip-audit, and bundle-audit check pinned versions against known CVEs. Pin then scan.

punchline.text: Pin versions, lock deps, review updates. Predictable builds are safer.

quiz.question: Why are lock files important for supply chain security?
options: They pin exact dependency versions and hashes to prevent surprise updates
They prevent developers from adding new dependencies
